title: Gentle Yeasty Filipino Spring Rolls
description: A light and shaggy Filipino dish featuring yeasty egg roll wrappers, this recipe is egg-free-friendly and perfect for a snack or dinner.
instructions: Combine yeast, warm water, and sugar in a bowl, then let it sit for 5 minutes until the yeast is activated.
Add flour, salt, and 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the bowl, then knead the mixture for 10 minutes until it forms a smooth dough.
Divide the dough into 20 equal pieces, then roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin sheet, about 1/16 inch thick.
Place a sheet of egg roll wrapper on a flat surface, then place a sheet of the yeasty dough on top of it.
Place 1 tablespoon of shredded cabbage and 1 tablespoon of grated carrots in the center of the dough.
Brush the edges of the dough with a little bit of water, then fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle or a square shape.
Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients.
Heat about 2 inches of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat, then fry the spring rolls until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the spring rolls from the oil, then place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
